meta.name: Seafood Chowder
meta.subtitle: Creamy seafood base • optional bacon/smoked notes • adaptable with what you have

source.notes: SSA baseline chowder: a flexible seafood chowder using a light roux + dairy. Supports optional bacon/smoked flavor and mixed seafood (shrimp, fish, scallops).

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on potato size and simmer.

tasteProfile.notes: Creamy, briny seafood flavor with tender potatoes. Optional bacon/smoked notes add depth.

ingredients.label: Bacon, diced (optional for smoky base)
ingredients.notes: About 3 slices. If omitted, start with butter/olive oil.

ingredients.label: Butter
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons. If using bacon, reduce butter to 15g.

ingredients.label: Dry white wine (optional)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Helps lift browned bits and adds brightness.

ingredients.label: Seafood stock or chicken broth (low-sodium preferred)
ingredients.notes: About 5 cups. Seafood stock is best; chicken broth works in a pinch.

ingredients.label: Clam juice (optional for stronger seafood flavor)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. If used, reduce broth by the same amount.

ingredients.label: Yukon gold or russet potatoes, diced
ingredients.notes: About 4 medium. Yukon gold stays creamy; russet breaks down more.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. For lighter chowder, use more milk and less cream.

ingredients.label: Corn (optional)
ingredients.notes: Frozen or canned; adds sweetness and color.

ingredients.label: Mixed seafood (shrimp + firm white fish + scallops optional)
ingredients.notes: Example: 250g shrimp + 350g cod/haddock. Add scallops if desired; see planner notes for timing.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 teaspoon; seafood stock can be salty—final-season at end.

ingredients.label: Fresh parsley or chives, chopped (optional garnish)

steps.title: Build the base (optional bacon)
instructions: If using bacon: add diced bacon to a cold pot and cook over medium heat until it renders and crisps (6–8 minutes).
Spoon off excess fat if needed, leaving ~1–2 tablespoons fat in the pot.
Add butter (reduce to ~15g if using bacon).

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Add onion and celery. Cook 6–8 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Make a roux
instructions: Sprinkle flour over the aromatics and stir well.
Cook 1–2 minutes, stirring, to remove raw flour taste (do not brown heavily).

steps.title: Deglaze and add stock
instructions: If using wine: pour it in and scrape the bottom of the pot; simmer 1 minute.
Slowly whisk in broth (and clam juice if using) until smooth.
Add potatoes, thyme, bay leaf, smoked paprika (optional), salt, and pepper.
Bring to a gentle simmer.

steps.title: Simmer until potatoes are tender
instructions: Simmer 15–20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender.
If you want a thicker chowder, lightly mash a cup of potatoes against the side of the pot and stir back in.

steps.title: Add dairy and gently heat
instructions: Lower heat to keep the soup below a boil.
Stir in milk and cream. Warm 3–5 minutes.

steps.title: Cook seafood (do not overcook)
instructions: Add fish pieces first (if using), simmer gently 2–3 minutes.
Add shrimp (and scallops if using) and cook 2–4 minutes more, just until opaque.
If using corn, stir it in during the last 2 minutes to warm through.

steps.title: Season and serve
instructions: Remove bay leaf (if used). Taste and adjust salt/pepper.
Add lemon juice (optional) to brighten.
Serve hot, topped with parsley/chives (optional).

seafoodOptions: Best mix: firm white fish (cod/haddock) + shrimp.
Scallops: add in the last 2–3 minutes.
Crab or lobster: add pre-cooked at the very end just to warm.
Canned clams: add at the end with a bit of the clam juice.
smokedNotes: No bacon? Use smoked paprika, smoked salt, or a tiny splash of liquid smoke (very little).
thicknessControl: Thicker: mash some potatoes, or increase flour to 50g.
dairyHandling: Avoid boiling after adding dairy to prevent curdling.
If your stock is very hot, lower heat before adding milk/cream.

bestPractices: Chowder is best fresh. For make-ahead: cook base through potatoes + dairy, then cool.
Add seafood only when reheating/serving for best texture.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 3 days. Seafood texture is best within 24–48 hours. Reheat gently; do not boil.
storage.freezer: Not ideal due to dairy + seafood texture changes, but the potato-dairy base can be frozen up to 2 months (add seafood fresh later).
storage.reheat: Warm over low heat, stirring. Add a splash of milk/broth if thickened.

notes: If using raw fish, cut into 1-inch chunks so it cooks evenly without falling apart.
Seafood cooks fast—keep heat gentle and stop as soon as shrimp are opaque.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions; no disallowed top-level properties.
